description:
src/lib.rs:138 'None | Some("latest") => SecretVersion::Latest' and :167 'SecretVersion::Latest => "latest"'. The string 'latest' is a wire-protocol value (sent in the URL path '.../versions/latest:access') and the URI default. Defining it as a constant makes the wire-protocol coupling explicit:
  const LATEST_VERSION: &str = "latest";

Suggested rewrite: replace both occurrences. Also documents the contract that SecretVersion::Latest's wire encoding is exactly 'latest' (not 'LATEST' or '*' or 'current').

Engineering basis: minor maintainer-burden — if GCP ever changed the default-version sentinel, two grep targets vs one constant. Trivial fix, free clarity.
title: Magic string 'latest' duplicated; not extracted to a constant
